name: Honey'n' Oat's Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, mix together the oats, buttermilk, egg, whole wheat flour, bread flour, and salt until combined.
2. Stir in the honey and yeast (or baking soda if making a one-pound loaf).
3. Transfer the batter to a greased bread pan or directly into a preheated 375°F (190°C) oven-proof Dutch oven with lid.
4. Cover with foil or place the lid on the dutch oven, and bake for 30 minutes until golden brown.
5. Remove from the oven and cool on a wire rack for at least one hour before slicing.
To make a one-pound loaf, use 1/3 cup oats, 3/4 cup buttermilk (or reconstituted dry buttermilk), 1 egg, 1 cup whole wheat flour, 1 cup bread flour, 1 tsp salt, and 1 tbsp honey. Adjust the yeast accordingly by using baking soda instead for a one-pound loaf.
